description: Fix all HIGH priority issues from GLM-5.1 code review — SEO, security, accessibility, content, performance

userStories.title: Add canonical URLs and Open Graph tags to all pages
userStories.description: As a site owner, I need canonical URLs and Open Graph tags so that search engines and social platforms correctly index and display my pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Every page (index, about, services, pricing, blog, contact) has <link rel="canonical"> with full URL
Every page has og:title, og:description, og:url, og:type, og:image meta tags
Every page has Twitter Card tags (twitter:card, twitter:title, twitter:description)
og:image uses a placeholder or PepeWebTech branded image reference
All tags validated with no duplicate or missing values
userStories.notes: Pages: index.html, about/index.html, services/index.html, pricing/index.html, blog/index.html, contact/index.html

userStories.title: Add JSON-LD structured data to homepage and blog posts
userStories.description: As a site owner, I need structured data so that search engines can better understand my content for rich results.
acceptanceCriteria: Homepage has Organization JSON-LD with name, url, logo, contact info
Homepage has WebSite JSON-LD schema
Blog posts have BlogPosting JSON-LD with title, datePublished, author, description
JSON-LD is valid (no syntax errors)
Structured data references correct URLs
userStories.notes: Blog posts are in blog/posts/ directory. Add to at least 5 recent posts as template.

userStories.title: Add security headers and caching to vercel.json
userStories.description: As a site owner, I need security headers and proper caching so that the site is secure and performs well.
acceptanceCriteria: X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff on all routes
X-Frame-Options: DENY on all routes
Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin on all routes
Permissions-Policy restricting camera, microphone, geolocation
Cache-Control with long max-age for /css/ and /js/ (immutable assets)
Duplicate outputDirectory key removed from vercel.json
vercel.json is valid JSON
userStories.notes: See code review Section 10 for exact header values.

userStories.title: Fix contact form — replace Formspree placeholder
userStories.description: As a visitor, I need the contact form to actually work so that I can reach PepeWebTech.
acceptanceCriteria: Form action points to a working endpoint OR use Web3Forms (free, no signup needed with form-id)
Form submission shows success message (not alert())
Form submission shows error message on failure
Client-side validation for required fields and email format
Form is accessible (aria-live for status messages)
userStories.notes: Web3Forms is free: https://web3forms.com/ — needs access key from Mr. Z. Alternatively, use a Supabase edge function.

userStories.title: Fix pricing inconsistency between Services and Pricing pages
userStories.description: As a potential client, I need consistent pricing across the site so that I don't get confused or lose trust.
acceptanceCriteria: Services page and Pricing page show the same prices for matching tiers
All pricing mentions across homepage, services, and pricing are consistent
Document the final agreed prices in pricing section of each page
userStories.notes: Services page says $3K/$5K, Pricing page says $4K/$8K. Pick one set and make consistent.

userStories.title: Add mobile-fixes.css to blog post pages
userStories.description: As a mobile visitor, I need blog posts to work properly on my phone with touch targets and safe area insets.
acceptanceCriteria: All blog post HTML files include <link rel="stylesheet" href="/css/mobile-fixes.css">
Blog posts use clamp() for padding-top instead of fixed 120px
Test at least 3 blog posts to confirm mobile-fixes apply
userStories.notes: Blog posts are in blog/posts/. There may be 60+ files. Use a script to add the link tag to all.

userStories.title: Extract blog post inline CSS into shared blog-post.css
userStories.description: As a developer, I need blog post styles in a shared CSS file so that changes don't require editing 60+ HTML files.
acceptanceCriteria: Create css/blog-post.css with all shared blog post styles
Remove inline <style> blocks from all blog post HTML files
Add <link rel="stylesheet" href="/css/blog-post.css"> to all blog posts
Blog posts still render identically after the extraction
userStories.notes: Use a script to automate the extraction across all blog posts.

userStories.title: Fix invalid nav HTML — wrap buttons in li elements
userStories.description: As a user relying on assistive technology, I need valid HTML so that screen readers correctly parse the navigation.
acceptanceCriteria: All <button> elements inside <ul> are wrapped in <li>
Navigation still works correctly (mobile toggle, theme toggle, close button)
No visual regression
HTML validates without block-level element inside inline element errors
userStories.notes: Applies to all pages. The nav structure has buttons for theme toggle, close menu that are direct children of <ul>.

userStories.title: Add skip-to-content link to all pages
userStories.description: As a keyboard user, I need a skip-to-content link so that I don't have to tab through the entire navigation on every page.
acceptanceCriteria: Skip-to-content link as first focusable element in each page
Link is visually hidden until focused (skip-link pattern)
Link jumps to main content area
Applied to all 6 main pages
userStories.notes: Add id="main" to main content wrapper if not already present.

userStories.title: Replace href="#" social links with real URLs
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want social links to actually work so that I can follow PepeWebTech on social media.
acceptanceCriteria: Twitter/X link points to @SpanglishGamer or remove if not ready
LinkedIn link points to Josue Zazueta's LinkedIn or remove if not ready
GitHub link points to webdevjosue GitHub
No href="#" links remain in the footer
If social accounts aren't ready, use aria-hidden to hide the links rather than pointing to #
userStories.notes: GitHub: https://github.com/webdevjosue. LinkedIn and X: ask Mr. Z for URLs or hide them.

userStories.title: Add aria-expanded and aria-hidden to mobile menu
userStories.description: As a screen reader user, I need ARIA states on the mobile menu so that I know when it's open or closed.
acceptanceCriteria: Mobile menu button has aria-expanded (true when open, false when closed)
Nav links container has aria-hidden (true when closed, false when open)
States update correctly when toggling menu
Applied to all pages with mobile menu
userStories.notes: JS toggle function needs to update these attributes.

userStories.title: Replace alert() with accessible inline messages in contact form
userStories.description: As a user, I need form feedback as inline messages instead of browser alerts for a better and more accessible experience.
acceptanceCriteria: Success state shows inline message (not alert())
Error state shows inline message with error details
Messages use aria-live for screen reader announcement
Form resets after successful submission

userStories.title: Fix pagination bug in blog filterAndDisplay function
userStories.description: As a blog visitor, I need pagination to work correctly when filtering categories so that the right number of posts appears.
acceptanceCriteria: filterAndDisplay() correctly counts visible items regardless of filter state
Page limit works correctly after filtering
Load More button shows/hides based on actual remaining items
No items are permanently hidden after filter switch
userStories.notes: The bug: filterAndDisplay counts visibleCount incrementally but hides items beyond page limit incorrectly.

userStories.title: Add noscript fallback for card visibility
userStories.description: As a user without JavaScript, I need cards to be visible so that the page content is not hidden.
acceptanceCriteria: <noscript> tag added with CSS rules to make service-cards, features, pricing-cards, blog-cards visible (opacity: 1)
Cards still animate in normally when JS is enabled
No visual difference for JS-enabled users
userStories.notes: Cards start with opacity:0 and IntersectionObserver makes them visible. Without JS, they stay invisible.

userStories.title: Add favicon to all pages
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to see a favicon in the browser tab so that I can identify the PepeWebTech tab easily.
acceptanceCriteria: Favicon added to all 6 main pages
Favicon works in modern browsers (favicon.ico and/or SVG)
If no favicon image exists yet, create a simple text-based favicon or use a placeholder path
userStories.notes: Can use a simple emoji favicon as placeholder: data:image/svg+xml,<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 100 100'><text y='.9em' font-size='90'>🛠️</text></svg>
